tw.photographer: K X I T H V I S U A L S
tw.description: It was a rainy and cold evening at the happening street market at Ningxia Road. Locals were browsing through the stalls down the road after working hours and just getting some dinner before heading home. With the savory aroma and smell of all the local delicacies, it was irresistible to not try some of them, except for the infamous stinky tofu. However, locals said it smells horrible but tastes like heaven. That doesn’t change my perspective on the stinky tofu anyway. This photo made me realized how much more Taiwan has to offer, especially when there is already so much great food around.

hk.description: A morning view of hongkong skyline from the peak
Check out my instagram to find more:) IG: nodefchilok

The iconic photo of Hong Kong, viewing from Avenue of Stars on Kowloon side of Victoria Harbour towards the office buildings in Wanchai harbour front. The tallest building is Central Plaza which was once the tallest building in HK before Ifc and ICC was built. The Chinese antique style junk boat provides harbour tour for tourists. Photo taken in late July 4p.m.

I was visiting my family in Kuala Lumpur and decided to go on a photo walk for the first time. This was taken from KL Tower, the tallest man made structure in Malaysia. The view at night is breathtaking and reminds me of how much more there is to see in the places we’ve been living in, but never really observing from a distance.

de.description: View over the River Spree to Nikolaiviertel and Alexanderplatz. The Nikolaiviertel is the reconstructed historical heart of the German capital Berlin. The Nikolaikirche (Saint Nicholas Church) gives its name to the neighbourhood five minutes away from Alexanderplatz. The Alexanderplatz is a large public square and transport hub in the central Mitte district of Berlin, near the Fernsehturm the Rotes Rathaus, the town hall of Berlin.
a train traveling down train tracks next to tall buildings

us.description: building beside body of water during night time
Summiting the Rockefeller Centre in NYC is expensive, but if you wait till it gets dark and if you're lucky with the weather, you can get some amazing shots at night. No helicopter needed.
This was taken from the roof of 375 Hudson in the West Village looking up toward Midtown on a cold January evening just after sunset.

My friends told me about the city of Hoboken in New Jersey and its beautiful surroundings. But I fell in love with the magestic view that city gave me. To find this place I have to walk around 10 minutes away from the Hoboken Train Station, for taking this pic I had to wait for about 2 hours, it was a cold and sunny afternoon and in fact I was waiting for the sunset and it’s magical color reflecting the skycrappers and buildings of New York City. In the end standing the cold for 2 hours was worth it!
